brief_justification: Arrives at exactly (C) with correct mapping of all six lag windows to month-6 status; goes three levels deep — identifies which effects materialize, explains the temporal mismatch mechanism, and draws the second-order 'worst sequencing' implication; proceeds linearly without naming biases or pausing to question its own method, though the structured enumeration implicitly forces completeness; checks each effect against its lag range and confirms the analyst/economist framing matches the derived timeline, but no counterfactual or boundary-condition test; uses hedged phrasing ('just beginning,' 'starting to materialize') for boundary-range effects but does not state confidence levels or name what would flip the conclusion; directly addresses the analyst's 'cancel out' claim and explains why it fails, but does not evaluate options A, B, or D by name; every conclusion is traceable — lag ranges are cited, month-6 status is derived explicitly for each effect, and a third party could reproduce the full reasoning chain without external context.
